what_is_open: Whether the hypothetical multiscale sequence \((C_n^{\mathrm{ms}})_n\) can be uniformly honest over \(\mathcal U\) while attaining expected length \(O(\delta_n)\) on \(\mathcal P_0\) and controlling the near-tie risk on \(\mathcal P_1\).
obstruction: The frozen multiscale route is only a construction handle: it does not specify an observable bias certificate or prove simultaneous coverage. Moreover, the parametric tie lower bound rules out \(O(\delta_n)\) whenever \(3s_1\alpha>d\), because then \(\delta_n=o(n^{-1/2})\).
attempted: The proved split-sample learned-policy interval was used as the explicit fallback. It supplies honest coverage through independent policy evaluation and a Markov-calibrated regret allowance, but its length has order \(n^{-1/2}+n^{-\rho}\), not generally \(\delta_n\).
partial_result: The explicit sequence \((C_n^\dagger)_n\) is finite-sample honest over \(\mathcal U\) and has uniform expected length \(O(n^{-1/2}+n^{-\rho})\) on both strata. Attainment of \(O(\delta_n)\) is impossible when \(3s_1\alpha>d\) and remains open in the nonexcluded regimes.

what_is_open: Whether the lower scale \(n^{-1/2}\vee n^{-\xi}\) is attainable throughout \(2s_1\alpha<d\), or whether a different least-favorable causal experiment raises the lower scale to the current achievable order \(n^{-\rho}\).
obstruction: The sparse randomized-sign experiment yields the exponent \(\xi\), while the explicit split-sample construction yields the smaller exponent \(\rho\). The current core contains neither a sharper honest upper construction nor a stronger indistinguishability experiment that closes this exponent gap.
attempted: The sparse-mixture and parametric local experiments were combined with the split-sample learned-policy interval. This closes the root-\(n\) subregime but leaves the two nonparametric regimes separated.
partial_result: For both strata, the minimax expected-length coefficient is bounded below at \(\ell_n\asymp n^{-\min\{1/2,\xi\}}\) and bounded above at \(u_n\asymp n^{-\min\{1/2,\rho\}}\). The scales match at root \(n\) when \(2s_1\alpha\ge d\); the regimes \(2s_1\alpha<d\le3s_1\alpha\) and \(3s_1\alpha<d\) remain open.
